title: pedicularis gracilis subsp. macrocarpa
intro: Pedicularis gracilis subsp. macrocarpa, also known as long-stalked lousewort, is a plant species in the bumblebee flower family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, although further research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea or tincture, with some traditional practices also using it in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
